experiments.hypothesis: Redirecting to a relevant tool page instead of generic /features should recover click-through from users searching for barcode/QR scanning tools
experiments.learnings: Early results (14d, 2026-03-05→2026-03-19): 10 clicks, 508 impr, 2.0% CTR, pos 9.2. vs baseline: CTR +23% (1.62→2.0), position improved 15.7→9.2. Impressions down (seasonal or Google re-evaluating). Redirect working as intended.

experiments.before: The Open Source Asset Tracking Platform | Shelf Asset Management
experiments.after: Open Source Asset Management Software — Free for Teams | Shelf Asset Management
experiments.hypothesis: 8,248 monthly impressions for 'asset management software' with 0.0% CTR. Current title says 'Asset Tracking' not 'Asset Management' and doesn't mention 'free'. Matching the search intent + adding 'free' should lift CTR.
experiments.learnings: Early results (14d, 2026-03-05→2026-03-19): 465 clicks, 35,073 impr, 1.3% CTR, pos 13.0. vs baseline: CTR +65% (0.79→1.3), position improved 16→13. Strong signal — 'Asset Management Software' + 'Free' in title is working. Homepage is the highest-leverage page.

experiments.before: Track equipment across people, locations, and workflows with QR-first simplicity, custody logging, and modern workflows.
experiments.after: Track who has what, where it is, and when it's due back. QR scanning, custody logs, and booking calendars — free for individuals, open source.
experiments.hypothesis: 5,372 impressions at 0.2% CTR and pos 30.9. More conversational tone + mentioning 'free' and 'open source' in the meta description should make the listing more clickable and differentiate from paid competitors.
experiments.learnings: Early results (14d, 2026-03-05→2026-03-19): 14 clicks, 2,012 impr, 0.7% CTR, pos 16.1. vs baseline: CTR +180% (0.25→0.7), position improved dramatically 30.3→16.1. Conversational description + 'free' + 'open source' is a winning formula. Biggest position jump of batch 1.

experiments.after: Equipment Check-In & Check-Out Software — QR Scan, Track, Return | Shelf
experiments.hypothesis: 6,877 impressions at 0.4% CTR and pos 19.0. Adding action verbs (scan, track, return) makes the listing more compelling and concrete in search results vs the generic current title.
experiments.learnings: Early results (14d, 2026-03-05→2026-03-19): 19 clicks, 2,839 impr, 0.7% CTR, pos 10.9. vs baseline: CTR +150% (0.28→0.7), position improved 19.2→10.9. Action verbs in title working. Page moved from page 2 to page 1.

experiments.before: No page exists — /macrs-depreciation-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Built dedicated interactive MACRS depreciation calculator tool page with 301 redirect
experiments.hypothesis: 8,320 monthly impressions going to a generic /tools page. Building a dedicated calculator should capture this traffic and feed users into the Shelf funnel via CTAs.
experiments.learnings: Page built in PR #14. GSC (30d): 138 clicks, 9,452 impr, 1.5% CTR, pos 11.6. Top query: 'macrs depreciation calculator' (25 clicks, pos 6.6). PostHog: 26 views/14d. GSC still reports old URL — 301 working but not yet re-indexed.

experiments.before: No page exists — /equipment-depreciation-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Built dedicated interactive equipment depreciation calculator tool page with 301 redirect
experiments.hypothesis: 7,494 monthly impressions going to generic /tools. Dedicated page with straight-line depreciation calculator should capture this traffic.
experiments.learnings: Page built in PR #14. GSC (30d): 27 clicks, 7,950 impr, 0.3% CTR, pos 16.8. Weakest performer of the 3 calculators — deeper position and lower CTR. May benefit from title/description optimization. PostHog: 7 views/14d.

experiments.before: No page exists — /salvage-value-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Built dedicated interactive salvage value calculator tool page with 301 redirect
experiments.hypothesis: 8,163 monthly impressions going to generic /tools. Dedicated calculator page should capture this high-volume traffic.
experiments.learnings: Page built in PR #14. GSC (30d): 73 clicks, 8,358 impr, 0.9% CTR, pos 9.3. Best CTR of the 3 calculators. Top query: 'salvage value calculator' (22 clicks, pos 2.2). PostHog: 8 views/14d.

experiments.before: Snipe-IT Alternative — Modern Open-Source Asset Management | Shelf
experiments.after: Snipe-IT vs Shelf — Which Open Source Asset Manager Fits Your Team? (2026)
experiments.hypothesis: 1,522 impressions at 0.9% CTR, pos 10.0. The 'vs' format with a year tag performs better for comparison queries — makes the listing feel like a current, objective comparison rather than biased marketing.
experiments.learnings: Title change was deployed in PR #33. Code already live — marking deployedAt retroactively.

experiments.after: Free Asset Tag Maker & Label Designer | Shelf
experiments.hypothesis: 128 clicks/30d from /asset-tag-maker (18K impr, pos 24.8) + 'asset tag generator free' (7 clicks, 19.4% CTR). Current title says 'Label Designer' but searchers use 'asset tag' terminology. Matching search intent should lift CTR on tag-related queries.
experiments.learnings: Title change was deployed in PR #33. Code already live — marking deployedAt retroactively.

experiments.before: Title: 'Equipment Management Software — Free QR Tracking & Scheduling | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free equipment management software with QR scanning, custody tracking, booking calendars, and location management. Open source. Used by 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Free Equipment Management Software — Open Source | Shelf' / Desc: 'Manage shared equipment with QR scanning, booking calendars, and custody tracking. Free for individuals, no per-asset pricing. Open source — used by 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.hypothesis: SERP analysis: Page 1 for 'equipment management software' is all paid enterprise tools (ToolSense, Fleetio, Cheqroom, Samsara). None are free/open-source. Leading with 'Free' and 'no per-asset pricing' differentiates from every competitor on page 1. Batch 1 learnings: 'free' + 'open source' in descriptions drove +150-180% CTR lifts.

experiments.before: Free, open-source asset management with QR tracking, custody logs, and booking calendars. Sign up and start tracking in minutes. AGPL licensed. 3,000+ teams.
experiments.after: Free, open-source asset management — cloud-hosted or self-host. QR tracking, custody logs, and booking calendars. Start tracking in minutes, no setup required. AGPL licensed. 3,000+ teams.
experiments.hypothesis: SERP analysis: Snipe-IT dominates 'open source asset management' at #1 but is self-host only (requires PHP/MySQL setup). Our cloud+self-host option is unique. Replaced jargon 'No DevOps required' with clearer 'Start tracking in minutes, no setup required'. CTR already 3.3% — targeting position improvement via relevance signal.

experiments.before: Title: 'Camera Equipment Check-Out Software — QR Checkout for Film & Media | Shelf' / Desc: 'Free camera equipment checkout software for film programs and media teams. QR scanning, booking calendars, kit tracking. Open source. Used by 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.after: Title: 'Camera Equipment Checkout Software — QR Scan & Book | Shelf' / Desc: 'Camera gear checkout for film programs, media departments, and production houses. QR scan, book, track custody — no sign-out sheets. Open source, free to start. 3,000+ teams.'
experiments.hypothesis: 3,988 impr (30d), 0.6% CTR, pos 18.1. Removed misleading 'Free for Teams' (free tier is single-user). Kept action verbs ('scan, book, track') and pain point ('no sign-out sheets') from exp-004 success (+150% CTR). 'Free to start' is accurate — free tier exists for individuals.
